title: paronychia tenella
intro: Paronychia tenella, also known as wild madder, is a perennial plant species belonging to the madder family (Rubiaceae) and native to North America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The plant is known for its various health benefits, including treating skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and acne, as well as supporting digestive health and reducing inflammation. The herbal preparations of Paronychia tenella include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
